title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire at Last Minute; Naval Blockade Remains in Force
detail: With the US-Iran ceasefire set to expire at 7:50 p.m. ET on April 21, 2026, President Trump announced a last-minute indefinite extension — averting what would have been the resumption of major military strikes. Trump posted on Truth Social: 'I have therefore directed our Military to continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able, and will therefore extend the Ceasefire until such time as their proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other.' Trump said the extension was made at Pakistan's request and described it as conditional on Iran submitting a credible diplomatic proposal. Crucially, the US naval blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz remains fully in force. The US military had by this point struck more than 8,000 Iranian targets including 130 vessels, according to Admiral Brad Cooper. Iran's Foreign Ministry expressed qualified acknowledgment of the extension but refused to withdraw its conditions: ending the blockade, releasing $6 billion in frozen assets, and nuclear program guarantees. The ceasefire — which began April 7 after 52 days of US-Israel strikes on Iran — entered an open-ended second phase with no formal Iranian commitment to new talks.

media.caption: Trump extends Iran ceasefire indefinitely hours before expiry; naval blockade remains in full force

title: Kevin Warsh Vows He Won't Be Trump's 'Sock Puppet' at Fed Chair Confirmation Hearing
detail: Trump's Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee on April 21, 2026, in a contentious hearing. In a notable exchange with Sen. John Kennedy (R-LA), Warsh vowed he would not be Trump's 'sock puppet,' and stated that Trump 'never asked me to pre-determine, commit, fix, or decide on any interest rate decision.' Warsh proposed major reforms he called 'regime change' at the Fed: fewer Federal Open Market Committee meetings per year (from 8 to fewer), a new inflation targeting framework, and greater Fed transparency about models and forecasts. Senators pressed Warsh on an undisclosed $100 million investment portfolio that raised conflict-of-interest questions. Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC) signaled he might block the committee vote over an open criminal investigation into outgoing Chair Jerome Powell related to building renovations. Warsh's confirmation path appears likely but not certain. If confirmed, he would inherit a Fed managing energy inflation from the Iran war, elevated interest rates, and growing recession risks — a far more volatile environment than in his previous Fed service (2006–2011). Outgoing Chair Powell, who had long clashed with Trump over rate cuts, was expected to leave upon Warsh's confirmation.

media.caption: Kevin Warsh Fed confirmation hearing: vows independence, proposes regime change; Tillis threatens block

title: Trump Approval Hits Second-Term Low: 35% Overall; Prices/Inflation Net Approval -46
detail: A Verasight poll (conducted April 10–14, n=1,514, margin of error ±2.6%) published April 21, 2026 by political analyst G. Elliott Morris found Trump's overall approval at 35% approve / 61% disapprove — a net rating of -26, down from -18 in January 2026. Most striking was the inflation/prices figure: just 26% approve of Trump's handling of prices and inflation, with 72% disapproving — a net of -46, the worst single-issue number tracked in the second term. Economic approval was approximately 30%, down from 38% in March. On the Iran war, 64% of Americans say the conflict is 'not worth the cost.' The generic congressional ballot showed Democrats +7 (50% vs. 43%). Border security remained the only issue where Trump polled net positive (+1). A concurrent NBC News poll confirmed these trends, with Trump at or near second-term lows on economic and foreign policy handling. The results underscore the political cost of elevated energy prices — nationally averaging $4.05/gallon — and growing public concern about the Iran war's economic impact. Trump's approval among self-identified Republicans remained elevated at approximately 81%.

media.caption: Verasight/NBC poll: Trump approval at second-term low, 35% overall; prices/inflation net -46

title: Trump on CNBC: 'Surprised' Stock Market Held Up; Oil at ~$90, Expected $200
detail: President Trump appeared live on CNBC's 'Squawk Box' at 8:30 a.m. ET on April 21, 2026 for a wide-ranging interview covering Iran, markets, tariffs, and the Federal Reserve. On the Iran war, Trump said he expected to 'end up with a great deal' and was 'surprised' by the stock market's resilience — he had anticipated the Dow dropping 20% and oil hitting $200/barrel, but oil was instead trading 'around $90' (significantly below the $116/barrel peak after the April 20 Touska seizure, as markets responded positively to the ceasefire extension). On the Federal Reserve, Trump discussed Kevin Warsh's nomination and his expectation of interest rate cuts once inflation settles. On tariffs, Trump said he imposed duties on Switzerland, Denmark, and other countries that 'were making significant profits' from US trade. He also mentioned Anthropic's potential Department of Defense contract as an example of American technology leadership. Trump's appearance was seen as a move to reassure markets and signal confidence in a diplomatic resolution with Iran. The interview came hours before he formally announced the ceasefire extension.

media.caption: Trump on CNBC Squawk Box: 'surprised' markets held up amid Iran war; oil at ~$90, not $200 as feared
